Pre-Test Checklist of Tools and Requirements

  • Pre-Testing PC
  • Pre-Testing MPC
  • Internet and LAN connection
  • Commissioning sheet access
  • Screwdriver set
  • Wire cutters
  • M3 Test Motor
  • M3 Motor extension cable
  • Test tail kicker mount
  • Microfiber Cloth
  • Alcohol
  • Soapy water
  • Camera Shims
  • Calibration cards
  • MTG or Pokémon card
  • Tail Kicker Shim
  • Sharpie
  • Sticky notes
  • Hopper Weight
  • Ratchet (optional)
  • Multi Meter (Potentially)
  • Test LED strip (Potentially)
  • Zip ties (Potentially)
  • Crimping tool (Potentially)
  • Double crimp connector (Potentially)
  • Label maker (Potentially)
  • File (Potentially)

Step 1 - Login and Setup

Take Assembled Scanner off the shelf. Plug in the power, camera, motherboard, and LAN cables into the MPC. Open the “TCG Dashboard” saved under the favorites on the google chrome browser. Login to info+comm2@tcgmachines.com and use the super-secret password... [controlled company credential].

Once in, the right side of the screen will display “Machines in HQ” with a list of the Ports and MAC addresses. The Pre-testing MPC will always have the address that ends in “E2-FC”. Click on this to be brought to its overview page then select the Assign tab. Ctrl F is the fastest way to find things

Here you will assign a MAC address to the account you are logged into and then run a software update.

Return to the previous page and select “Local MPC Login” Here you will be prompted with the same login as before, using the same credentials as before. Once logged in, you will be brought to the Main interface for the Phyzbatch. On the right side of the screen click on the profile bubble “CG” and go to “Systems check page”

Step 2 - Motor and LED Checks

In the systems check page we will start by testing the Feed motor, positioning motor, and Conveyer motors as well as the Run, Illumination and Foil LEDs respectively. The testing conveyer motor will need to be plugged in to check if it works. If they all fire correctly and have no issues, move on to the next stage. Any issues will have to be addressed on a case-by-case basis. New machines generally have less issues. Refurbs often have issues mostly motor or wire connection related.

Step 3 - Cleaning Feed Wheels and Exit O-Ring Alignment

Cleaning is easiest when the “Shroud of Darkness” and “Chamber of Darkness” are out of the scanner, so remove them first. Grab a Microfiber cloth, rubbing alcohol, soapy water and gloves.

Make sure the “Phantom glass holder” isn’t in the scanner. As it will make the wheels inaccessible. Wet the cloth with alcohol and start the position motor. Use your index finger to press the cloth with medium force into the wheels, respraying the cloth when needed. If it sounds like a cat dying, you're doing it correctly. The wheels will always mark the cloth; this is just to mitigate how bad it is. Do all 4 wheels with alcohol then switch to soapy water and repeat the process. When finished, move on to aligning the O-rings on the left side. Using the O-ring tool push the O-rings into the grooves and make sure they are straight and don’t wobble. Shut off the position motor when finished.

Step 4 - Camera Setup / Focus

Next, check and setup the camera. New machines often have issues with the cameras due to either a warped camera board or uneven 3D printed Camera base. Under the optics tab you will see “Capture Fisheye” and “Capture empty dock” click each to take a picture of the inside of the scanner.

We are looking for uneven photos, either the left or right side will be overshot, to fix this remove the camera (4 M2x6 SOC). Using a small ratchet for the M2’s is recommended, then add two camera shims on the side that is overshot. Use the green diffuser panels as a reference point.

Next up is focusing the camera. Grab a “Phantom Glass Holder” with clean test glass and insert it into the scanner. Grab your favorite Pokémon or Magic card and slide it into the “feed fixer” preferably the top of the card facing you. While pushing up on the glass holder to avoid the card being damaged or getting jammed select “load card into dock” which can be found under the “Feed cards tab” take a new “Empty dock photo” with the card now visible loosen the screw that locks the lens in place DO NOT TAKE THE SCREW OUT you will regret being alive if you have to put it back. You can always turn on the illumination LED to see inside the scanner.

Adjust the lens by turning it left or right. The lens 9/10 times has to be turned left. Put the “Shroud of Darkness” back in and take a picture to check how in focus the image is. When the corners of the image are in good enough focus, tighten the screw and put the “Chamber of darkness” back. While pushing the “Phantom Glass” up select “Clear” to eject the card. This once again is to avoid jams.

Step 5 - Camera Calibration

Up next is the camera calibration. Start by checking the “Feed fixer” play; it should only have a little play horizontally using an 8 T to adjust if necessary. Once done, grab a calibration card and slide it into the “feed fixer” it should slide in with zero resistance. A good strategy is to tighten the “Feed fixer” until the first card can't go in, then loosen it off until it slides in with ease. This will set it up correctly so that a second card won't go in easily. If the second card goes too easily, the scanner will often double feed.

Once this is finished, attach the “Test tail kicker mount” with no screws and put a pack of calibration cards into the hopper. Before starting the calibration make sure the “Camera housing cover” is put in to keep the “Phantom Glass holder” in place, without it the scanner will jam and destroy the cards. As well as the conveyer motor must be plugged in.

Select the Camera calibration button, using your hand to catch the cards as they come out of the scanner. Issues to look for will be double feeding, Phantom Glass position i.e. jamming and photo sensors being dead or wired backwards. When the calibration is finished, it will update the “Empty dock image” If the image looks good and is still in focus and centered. Proceed to adjust the Dock Center and Glass edges. The top should line up with the black edge of “Phantom glass holder” The bottom line should be flush with the glass tab. Once the top and bottom are in place, adjust the middle line so that the left and right sides line up with the edge of the position rollers.

Housing Kit and Scanner Housing

Parts / Hardware

  • The Scanner is now finishing its first pre-test and will now need to be housed. You will need:
  • Half-assembled housing
  • Back housing panel
  • Cover front door
  • Backside Vent
  • Hopper wedge
  • Dream catcher
  • MPC wiring bracket (USB, RHS, POWER, BASE)
  • MPC Wi-Fi antenna housing top and base
  • MPC power supply
  • MPC
  • Static brush (If it's not already on the scanner)
  • Ziptie (Potentially)
  • Logo plate
  • Tail Kicker X2
  • Tail kicker mount
  • Exit card cover top and bottom

Hardware M3x6 BUT (2), M4x6 SOC (1), M3x6 Flat (4), M3x8 Flat (28?), M3x12 Flat (2), M6x14 BUT (3), M3x8 SOC (2), M3x8 BUT, M4x8 BUT

Take off the sticker from the power supply and MPC and stick them on the motherboard plate and write the MAC and MPC S/N on a sticky note. Start the housing by attaching the MPC wiring bracket base onto the MPC (M4x6 SOC and 3 M3x6 Flat). Plug the MPC power supply LAN into the MPC as well as the POWER Wiring bracket attached to the power cable and securing with (M3x6 Flat). Laying the MPC Power supply with its LAN port facing the same direction as the logo of the MPC.

Lay the scanner on the top of the MPC Power Supply and plug in the USB and RHS wiring bracket using (2 M3x6 Flat) to secure. Remove the screws from the LAN port before you put it in the housing.

Pick up the unit (Scanner, MPC with its power supply) to place it into the housing while keeping the wires below the PEMs; the walls may need to be bent back slightly to get it in. Make sure Scanner sits above the PEMs and the MPC and power supply sits below. It will click into place when done correctly. You can now screw the LAN port screws back in from where you got them as well as the frontside screws that are under the scanner door 3 (M3x8 Flat),

Next, take the Logo plate and secure it into the MPC Power supply through the metal housing with 2 (M3x8 BUT). You might need to move the MPC power supply around to get the threads to line up. It might be difficult to move around. I recommend brute force and ignorance.

Grab the MPC housing side back panel vent and screw it into the housing with 5 (M3x8 Flat) Then the backside panel with 10 (M3x8 Flat).

Place the “Dream catcher” on the top and line up the Wi-Fi antenna into the cutout on the “Dream catcher”

Next add the “Cover Front Door” secure with 8 (M3x8 Flat).

Up next add the Wi-Fi antenna clicking it into the top of the Wi-Fi housing, then sandwiching it to the base below. Put a M3x6 Flat into the M3 Nut and 2 (M3x12) Through the top into the scanner.

Add the Hopper wedge with 3 (M6x14)

Add exit card cover top and bottom 2 (M4x8) 2 (M3x8 Flat)

Finally add Tail kicker mount M3x8 SOC, M3x16 Flat with tail kickers and set with tail kicker shim

Final Test and Check

Once fully housed plug in the scanner power and LAN in, open The TCG Dashboard and ctrl-f to find the MAC address of the MPC you put into that scanner. Commission and update the scanner in the assign page to info+comm2@tcgmachines.com Head to systems check page, retake the “fisheye” and “empty dock image” and recalibrate the camera with the calibration cards and realign the center and edges. When finished, deliver the scanner to testing.

Sub-Assembly List / 3DP Upkeep Per Week

  • MPC Power supply x 10 SA
  • Housing pre-assembly x 10 SA
  • Scanner housing base x 10 SA
  • MPC Housing support plate x 10 PA
  • Frontside vent x 10 SA
  • Backside vent x 10 SA
  • Dream catcher x 10 SA
  • Dream catcher mount x 10 PA
  • Static brush x 10 SA
  • Hopper wedge x 10 PA
  • Adjustable soft mount bracket x 10
  • Soft mount washer PA
  • Soft mount PA
  • Phantom glass holder x 10 SA
  • Tail mount x 10 SA
  • Tail kickers x 20 SA
  • MPC Base wiring Bracket Base x 10 PA
  • Lower I/O Bracket x 10 PA
  • Upper I/O Bracket x 10 PA
  • Rear LAN port x 10 PA
  • 5G antenna base x 10 PA
  • MPC housing side panel upper back mount... Whoever named this should be fired x 10 PA
  • MPC side panel bracket x 30 PA
  • Camera housing cover x 10
